propositions: A single plan defect currently produces multiple blocking findings via multi-criterion co-citation, inflating the block wall and distorting per-criterion tallies.
norm_id is criterion-scoped (bakes sorted criteria into its hash) so it cannot group findings across criteria; a criteria-free key is required.
Add fix_unit_key(finding) = sha256(_norm_location + '|' + _norm_claim) using token-set normalization that excludes the criteria list.

Group blocking findings sharing a fix_unit_key by additively stamping group_id, is_primary (exactly one per group with deterministic tiebreak), and group_criteria (union), without removing any finding from verdict['blocking'].
Persist group_id, is_primary, and group_criteria through _slim()/build_payload so the stamps survive into the sidecar payload.
The CLI renderer prints only primary findings with a '(+N co-criteria: ...)' suffix, while library/MCP consumers see the full unsuppressed blocking list; decide.py is unchanged.
